_comment_nist_fields: Fields below follow IR 8278Ar1 Section 3.2 OLIR template
focal_document_element_description: Knowledge of data communications terminology (e.g.,  networking protocols, Ethernet, IP, encryption, optical devices, removable media).
reference_document_element_description: Knowledge of networking fundamentals relevant to data systems, including TCP/IP, DNS resolution, HTTP/HTTPS protocols, firewall and security group configurations, load balancing, and how network architecture affects data pipeline design and performance.
comments: DCWF ksa superset of WIDAI TF domain. TF-K-013 is the strongest semantic match. Secondary: AI-K-020 (AI, cosine: 0.572), DQ-K-014 (DQ, cosine: 0.569).
_comment_widai_extensions: Fields below are WIDAI extensions per ADR-014

primary_method.name: Cross-Encoder STS
primary_method.description: Semantic Textual Similarity via cross-encoder regression trained on STS-Benchmark.
primary_method.significance: STS 7.0/10 (raw 0.697). Shared vocabulary: [data, ip, knowledge, networking, protocols]. DCWF-only terms [communications, devices, encryption, ethernet, media, optical] vs WIDAI-only terms [affects, architecture, balancing, configurations, design, dns] define the semantic distance between the descriptions.

bertscore.significance: BERTScore F1=0.875 (P=0.884, R=0.866). Balanced P/R — the 15-word DCWF element and 31-word WIDAI KSA cover each other's token-level semantic content symmetrically.

nli_cross_encoder.significance: NLI: neutral-dominant (93.2%). "Knowledge of data communications terminology (e.g.,  networking protocols, Ether" and "Knowledge of networking fundamentals relevant to data systems, including TCP/IP," are related but neither implies nor contradicts the other. Standard result for cross-framework mapping where two independent frameworks describe overlapping competencies with independent vocabulary.

biencoder_cosine.description: Bi-encoder sentence embeddings with cosine similarity.
biencoder_cosine.significance: Bi-encoder 0.573 < STS 0.697 (gap -0.123). Cross-attention finds deeper alignment than independent encoding. The relationship depends on cross-text reasoning — concept-level mapping between [communications, devices, encryption] and [affects, architecture, balancing] that bi-encoders structurally cannot capture.

jaccard_token.description: Token-level Jaccard similarity (intersection/union of lowercased word tokens).
jaccard_token.significance: Jaccard 15.0%. Shared tokens: [data, ip, knowledge, networking, protocols]. DCWF-only: [communications, devices, encryption, ethernet, media, optical]. WIDAI-only: [affects, architecture, balancing, configurations, design, dns].
